summary: PR 2472 added a decompilation of src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94.c centered on hsd_803A949C and exposed its prototype in hsd_3A94.h. The implementation defines local card-state/command/block structs, helper routines for command entry lookup, retrying CARDClose, icon-size selection, and card-block file-id extraction, then handles multiple command cases for memory-card read/verify/status flows. The only loaded comment is an automated decomp report showing hsd_803A949C improved from 0.00% to 58.78%, not a full match claim.

key_files.role: Adds the hsd_803A949C implementation plus local structs and static helper functions for card command callback handling.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_3A94.c includes hsd_3A94.h, hsd_3AA7.h, hsd_3B2B.h, hsd_3B2E.h, dolphin/card.h, string.h
raw_diff: static helpers hsd_803A949C_GetEntry, hsd_803A949C_Close, hsd_803A949C_IconSize, hsd_803A949C_FileId
raw_diff: void hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1) with switch on entry->x0

key_files.role: Adds the public prototype for hsd_803A949C.

what_changed.text: Created the previously empty hsd_3A94.c body for hsd_803A949C, using local struct definitions to describe card state, command entry, and card block layouts.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_3A94.c changes from one blank line to 492 lines

what_changed.text: Factored repeated operations into static helpers: command-entry lookup through hsd_804D1148/hsd_804D7980, CARDClose retry loop, icon-size mapping from state->x3B0, and a two-byte card-block file id calculation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_803A949C_Close retries CARDClose while result == -1 and i < 10
raw_diff: hsd_803A949C_IconSize switch returns 0x1800 for case 2, 0xE00 for case 1, otherwise 0

what_changed.text: Implemented a large command dispatcher over entry->x0 with cases 1, 2, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 13, 15, and 16, using CARD close handling, validation helpers hsd_803B31CC/hsd_803B2B20, memcpy/memcmp, slot state updates, and final queue/index cleanup.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: calls hsd_803B31CC, hsd_803B2B20, memcpy, memcmp, hsd_803AC558
raw_diff: hsd_3A94.h adds /* 3A949C */ void hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1);

what_changed.text: Automated report recorded partial decomp progress for hsd_803A949C, improving from 0.00% to 58.78% and adding 2852 bytes in main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report for GALE01 shows hsd_803A949C +2852 bytes, Before 0.00%, After 58.78%

smart_moves.text: Used temporary typed structs with offset comments instead of expressing the whole function as raw byte-offset pointer math.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: CardStateExt fields include x0, x8, xC, x20, x24, x170, x270, x370, x3B0, x430, x460
raw_diff: CardCmdEntry fields include state and x8/xC/x10/x14/x18/x20
raw_diff: CardBlock fields include x10, x11, x12, x20

smart_moves.text: Extracted repeated computations and behaviors into small static helpers, making the large dispatcher easier to review.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_803A949C_Close used throughout error and normal paths
raw_diff: hsd_803A949C_IconSize reused in cases 10 and 11
raw_diff: hsd_803A949C_FileId reused in cases 5 and 13

smart_moves.text: Updated the owning header with the recovered function prototype rather than leaving the function only source-local.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For large callback/dispatcher functions, introducing narrow local structs with offset-preserving fields can turn repeated raw card-buffer and command-entry accesses into reviewable typed accesses while semantics are still incomplete.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_803A949C uses state->x24, state->x8, entry->x10, block->x12 instead of only raw offsets

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a dispatcher repeats small operations such as close/retry, icon-size lookup, or ID extraction across multiple cases, factoring those into static helpers can clarify structure without requiring fully semantic global names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: helper calls appear in multiple switch cases

decomp_lessons.lesson: A PR can land useful partial progress without claiming a full match; intake should preserve the bot-reported percent rather than converting it into a matched-symbol claim.

naming_conventions.convention: The exported function and static helpers keep the address-style hsd_803A949C prefix, with semantic suffixes only for local helper roles such as GetEntry, Close, IconSize, and FileId.

Incomplete struct fields retain offset-like names with offset comments, while clear local variables use role names such as entry, state, block, result, offset, remaining, data_offset, hdr_plus_icon, slot, and sector_size.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct fields x0, x8, x24, x3B0, x430 with offset comments
raw_diff: local variables entry, state, block, result, icon_size, offset, remaining, data_offset, hdr_plus_icon, slot, sector_size

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used a do-while loop for the CARDClose retry behavior, retrying while result is -1 and the count is below 10.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_803A949C_Close initializes i = 0 and uses do { result = CARDClose(...); ... i++; } while (i < 10)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used a switch over entry->x0 for adjacent command cases instead of a generated branch ladder.
Centralized common end-of-callback queue cleanup after the switch.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: after switch, entry->x0 = 0; hsd_804D799C = 0; hsd_804D7980 = (hsd_804D7980 + 1) % 128

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used explicit casts for command-entry payload fields when they serve as destination/source pointers for memcpy and memcmp.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use offset-preserving temporary structs to make large dispatcher decompilation reviewable
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2472, hsd_803A949C introduced local CardStateExt, CardCmdEntry, and CardBlock structs with offset comments and used them throughout a large card-command dispatcher. This supports a reusable candidate lesson: when real global types are not fully recovered, narrow temporary structs can improve reviewability over raw pointer math while preserving unknown field names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_803A949C accesses state, entry, and block fields throughout switch cases

accepted_candidate_records.title: Do not inflate partial score reports into full match claims
accepted_candidate_records.text: The only validation-like evidence for PR 2472 is the bot report showing hsd_803A949C improved from 0.00% to 58.78%. Curated records should retain that as partial decomp progress unless separate objdiff/build evidence is found.

source_update_candidates.title: Temporary local structs can be acceptable bridge code when fields are offset-preserving and semantics are not proven

This aligns with typed-fields-over-pointer-math and conservative-naming but may be worth a small explicit note if repeated across PRs.
